name: Simplot
description_short: Simplot Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering auto loans, mortgages, checking/savings accounts, and credit cards with competitive rates and nationwide ATM access.
description_long:
Simplot Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution that serves individuals seeking community-focused banking alternatives to traditional for-profit banks. The credit union operates under NCUA insurance and emphasizes member benefits through competitive loan rates, deposit products, and personalized financial services.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (starting at 4.99% APR as of January 2025), RV loans, mortgages, signature loans, and credit cards. On the deposit side, Simplot provides checking and savings accounts featuring the Kasasa Rewards program, which offers nationwide ATM fee refunds, zero monthly fees, and no minimum balance requirements. They also offer certificates of deposit (CDs) and individual retirement accounts (IRAs) with competitive yields, plus online loan payment processing and financial calculators.

Simplot distinguishes itself through its Kasasa Rewards checking account, which provides cash-back rewards, nationwide ATM access without fees, and no minimum balance—appealing to fee-conscious members. The credit union emphasizes digital accessibility with mobile apps for iOS and Android, online banking enrollment, and online loan applications. Their focus on member feedback and community involvement (evident from the "Member Feedback" option and mission-driven approach) reflects traditional credit union values.

As a credit union, Simplot's primary limitation is membership eligibility—not all consumers can join without meeting specific field-of-membership requirements. The posted rates on their website show modest yields on savings products (0.15% APY on regular shares) compared to some online banks, though this is typical for credit unions. The institution appears mid-sized based on service scope, serving a regional membership rather than national banking operations.
pros: Auto loans available at competitive rates starting at 4.99% APR
Kasasa Rewards checking account with nationwide ATM fee refunds and zero monthly fees
No minimum balance requirement for rewards on checking accounts
Free mobile banking apps available for iOS and Android
Mortgage lending services with flexible terms
Share certificates (CDs) offering up to 4.75% APY
Member-owned structure means profits return to members rather than shareholders
cons: Membership eligibility requirements limit access—not all consumers can join
Savings account yields (0.15% APY) are modest compared to online-only banks
Limited information provided about specific membership eligibility criteria on website
RV loan rates (7.24% APR) are notably higher than auto loan rates
No mention of personal loan amounts, terms, or rates on their website
best_for: Individuals seeking affordable auto financing within an existing credit union membership
Members prioritizing fee-free checking with nationwide ATM access and rewards
Homebuyers looking for mortgage options from a community-focused financial institution
Savers interested in competitive CD rates through a not-for-profit structure
diagnosis: Simplot Credit Union is best for members seeking competitive loan rates and fee-free checking with rewards within a not-for-profit, community-focused institution. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility requirements apply—prospective customers must qualify for membership based on field-of-membership criteria before accessing services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Simplot — NCUA-insured credit union in POCATELLO, ID. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
